div.head: Mr. Alexander C. Kirk, United States Political Adviser on the Staff of the Supreme Allied Commander, Mediterranean Theater (Wilson), to the Secretary of State
div.p: 484. My 324, September 7, midnight, second paragraph. On instructions from SACMED member of Maclean’s Mission recently interviewed TitoMarshal Josip Broz-Tito, Commander of the Yugoslav National Army of Liberation, and President of the National Committee of Liberation of Yugoslavia. with view to obtaining latter’s agreement to application of principles governing surrender of Germans recently enunciated by CCS. Tito fully agreed and is issuing instructions to his corps commanders accordingly. It is reported that he proposes to place Germans surrendering in concentration camps for road work in Yugoslavia.
